      (function () {
        const theme = localStorage.getItem("theme") || "system";
        const root = document.documentElement;
        const mediaQuery = window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)");
        const faviconLink = document.getElementById("favicon");

        function applyTheme(doc) {
          const isDark =
            theme === "dark" || (theme === "system" && mediaQuery.matches);
          if (isDark) {
            doc.classList.add("dark");
            if (faviconLink) faviconLink.href = "/favicon-light.svg";
          } else {
            doc.classList.remove("dark");
            if (faviconLink) faviconLink.href = "/favicon-dark.svg";
          }
        }

        applyTheme(root);

        if (theme === "system") {
          mediaQuery.addEventListener("change", function () {
            applyTheme(root);
          });
        }

        document.addEventListener("astro:before-swap", function (e) {
          const theme = localStorage.getItem("theme") || "system";
          const isDark =
            theme === "dark" || (theme === "system" && mediaQuery.matches);
          if (isDark) {
            e.newDocument.documentElement.classList.add("dark");
          } else {
            e.newDocument.documentElement.classList.remove("dark");
          }
        });
      })();

d="M433.5,46.33c.59,7.61-3.38,28.05.25,33.06,1.52,2.29,6.62,2.41,7.97.46.98-1.67,3.07-1.96,3.12-.06-1.88,11.84-22.1,16.72-21.41-4.29h-.38c-2.81,8.3-11.09,14.9-20.14,14.71-6.35,0-11.55-3.03-11.47-9.76.31-13.24,18.07-20.57,31.36-24.86.69-8.46.31-21.3-10.58-21.34-8.28.02-12.41,6.42-11.22,14.71-2.03,2.06-5.38,1.48-7.71-.13-3.77-2.82-2.11-9.12,1.47-12.04,11.41-10.11,39.52-10.86,38.75,9.57ZM408.9,83.03c9.57-.94,13.51-13.03,13.89-23.69-7.83,3.09-13.92,5.74-17.78,11.52-2.72,4.29-2.75,12.37,3.89,12.17Z"></path> <path class="cls-1 letter-i" d="M450.55,88.76c-1.12-.29-1.15-3.45-.26-3.91,0,0,7.39,0,7.39,0,1.91,0,2.36-1.96,2.36-3.44,0-4.57.32-36.26,0-39.25-.45-4.18-.45-5.55-4.02-5.55,0,0-5.74,0-5.74,0-1.09-.12-1.51-3.74-.26-3.91,7.24.33,12.96-2.47,18.23-4.68,1.27,0,1.78.52,1.78,1.56-.08,23.45-.05,23.62,0,47.65,0,6.6-.43,7.62,2.68,7.62,0,0,6.25,0,6.25,0,1.2.1,1.42,3.41.26,3.64l-28.68.26Z"></path> <circle class="cls-1 letter-i-dot" cx="461.69" cy="17.53" r="6.26"></circle> </svg> <canvas id="vals-canvas-baxdvd" class="absolute inset-0 w-full h-full opacity-0 pointer-events-none" aria-hidden="true" style="image-rendering: pixelated;"></canvas> </span> <script data-astro-exec="">(function(){const svgId = "vals-logo-baxdvd";
const canvasId = "vals-canvas-baxdvd";
const wrapperId = "vals-logo-wrapper-baxdvd";
const maxSize = 6;
const duration = 800;

  (() => {
    const init = () => {
      const wrapper = document.getElementById(wrapperId);
      const svg = document.getElementById(svgId);
      const canvas = document.getElementById(canvasId);
      if (!wrapper || !svg || !canvas) return;

      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
      let srcW = 0,
        srcH = 0,
        dpr = 1;
      let textData = null,
        textSourceCanvas = null,
        iconCanvas = null;
      let srcFill = "black";
      let capturePromise = null;

      const easeInOut = (t) => (t < 0.5 ? 2 * t * t : -1 + (4 - 2 * t) * t);

      // Text letter x-regions in SVG viewBox (0 0 480 100), right-to-left:
      // i+i-dot, second-a, period, s, l, first-a, v
      const SEGMENTS = [
        { svgX: 444, svgW: 41 },
        { svgX: 385, svgW: 59 },
        { svgX: 367, svgW: 18 },
        { svgX: 313, svgW: 54 },
        { svgX: 279, svgW: 34 },
        { svgX: 225, svgW: 54 },
        { svgX: 152, svgW: 73 },
      ];
      const N_SEG = SEGMENTS.length;
      // Each segment takes 40% of t=0..1; stagger so last starts at t=0.6
      const segWidth = 0.4;
      const stagger = (1 - segWidth) / (N_SEG - 1); // ≈ 0.1

      const buildClone = () => {
        const clone = svg.cloneNode(true);
        clone.setAttribute("width", srcW);
        clone.setAttribute("height", srcH);
        const origEls = [...svg.querySelectorAll("[class]")];
        const cloneEls = [...clone.querySelectorAll("[class]")];
        origEls.forEach((el, i) => {
          const fill = getComputedStyle(el).fill;
          if (fill && fill !== "none") cloneEls[i].style.fill = fill;
        });
        return clone;
      };

      const renderClone = (clone) =>
        new Promise((resolve) => {
          const svgStr = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(clone);
          const blob = new Blob([svgStr], {
            type: "image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8",
          });
          const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
          const img = new Image();
          img.onload = () => {
            const off = document.createElement("canvas");
            off.width = srcW;
            off.height = srcH;
            off.getContext("2d").drawImage(img, 0, 0, srcW, srcH);
            URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
            resolve(off);
          };
          img.onerror = () => resolve(null);
          img.src = url;
        });

      const captureAll = async () => {
        const rect = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
        if (rect.width === 0) return;
        dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
        srcW = Math.round(rect.width * dpr);
        srcH = Math.round(rect.height * dpr);
        canvas.width = srcW;
        canvas.height = srcH;

        const clsEl = svg.querySelector(".cls-1");
        if (clsEl) srcFill = getComputedStyle(clsEl).fill;

        // Text-only: hide VA icon group
        const textClone = buildClone();
        const vaForText = textClone.querySelector("[clip-path]");
        if (vaForText) vaForText.setAttribute("visibility", "hidden");

        // Icon-only: hide text letters, remove clip so full VA mark renders
        const iconClone = buildClone();
        [
          ".letter-v",
          ".letter-a",
          ".letter-l",
          ".letter-s",
          ".letter-period",
          ".letter-second-a",
          ".letter-i",
          ".letter-i-dot",
        ].forEach((sel) => {
          const el = iconClone.querySelector(sel);
          if (el) el.setAttribute("visibility", "hidden");
        });
        const vaForIcon = iconClone.querySelector("[clip-path]");
        if (vaForIcon) vaForIcon.removeAttribute("clip-path");

        const [tc, iconCnv] = await Promise.all([
          renderClone(textClone),
          renderClone(iconClone),
        ]);
        textSourceCanvas = tc;
        textData = tc
          ? tc.getContext("2d").getImageData(0, 0, srcW, srcH).data
          : null;
        iconCanvas = iconCnv;
      };

      const ensureAll = () => {
        if (!capturePromise) capturePromise = captureAll();
        return capturePromise;
      };

      new MutationObserver(() => {
        capturePromise = null;
        textData = null;
        textSourceCanvas = null;
        iconCanvas = null;
      }).observe(document.documentElement, {
        attributes: true,
        attributeFilter: ["class"],
      });

      // t: 0→1. Segment i starts at t = i*stagger, completes over segWidth.
      // Each segment pixelates then fades out. VA icon stays crisp throughout.
      const FADE_START = 0.55; // fraction of segT when fade begins (after pixelation)
      const render = (t) => {
        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, srcW, srcH);

        // Base: crisp full text as foundation
        if (textSourceCanvas) ctx.drawImage(textSourceCanvas, 0, 0);

        // Overdraw each segment with pixelation + fade as it animates
        if (textData) {
          for (let si = 0; si < N_SEG; si++) {
            const segT = Math.max(
              0,
              Math.min((t - si * stagger) / segWidth, 1),
            );
            if (segT <= 0) continue;

            const p = easeInOut(segT);
            const bs = Math.max(1, Math.round((1 + (maxSize - 1) * p) * dpr));

            // Fade out the segment after FADE_START
            const segAlpha =
              segT >= FADE_START
                ? easeInOut(1 - (segT - FADE_START) / (1 - FADE_START))
                : 1;

            // Nothing to draw yet (still crisp with no fade)
            if (bs <= 1 && segAlpha >= 1) continue;

            const { svgX, svgW } = SEGMENTS[si];
            const x0 = Math.round((svgX / 480) * srcW);
            const x1 = Math.min(srcW, Math.round(((svgX + svgW) / 480) * srcW));

            // Clear base text in this region; blocks (or nothing) replace it
            ctx.clearRect(x0, 0, x1 - x0, srcH);

            if (segAlpha > 0.01 && bs > 1) {
              ctx.save();
              ctx.globalAlpha = segAlpha;
              ctx.fillStyle = srcFill;
              for (let y = 0; y < srcH; y += bs) {
                for (let x = x0; x < x1; x += bs) {
                  let a = 0,
                    n = 0;
                  for (let dy = 0; dy < bs && y + dy < srcH; dy++) {
                    for (
                      let dx = 0;
                      dx < bs && x + dx < x1 && x + dx < srcW;
                      dx++
                    ) {
                      a += textData[((y + dy) * srcW + (x + dx)) * 4 + 3];
                      n++;
                    }
                  }
                  if (n > 0 && a / n > 102)
                    ctx.fillRect(x, y, Math.min(bs, x1 - x), bs);
                }
              }
              ctx.restore();
            }
            // segAlpha ≤ 0.01: region cleared, letter is gone
          }
        }

        // Icon always crisp on top
        if (iconCanvas) ctx.drawImage(iconCanvas, 0, 0);
      };

      const hoverTarget = wrapper.closest("a") ?? wrapper;
      let animFrame = null;
      let animT = 0;

      const animateTo = (target, onDone) => {
        if (animFrame) cancelAnimationFrame(animFrame);
        const from = animT;
        const dist = Math.abs(target - from);
        if (dist < 0.001) {
          render(animT);
          return onDone?.();
        }
        const dur = duration * dist;
        const start = performance.now();
        const tick = (now) => {
          const rawT = Math.min((now - start) / dur, 1);
          animT = from + (target - from) * rawT;
          render(animT);
          if (rawT < 1) {
            animFrame = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
          } else {
            animT = target;
            onDone?.();
          }
        };
        animFrame = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
      };

      const onEnter = async () => {
        await ensureAll();
        svg.style.opacity = "0";
        canvas.style.opacity = "1";
        animateTo(1);
      };

      const onLeave = () => {
        animateTo(0, () => {
          canvas.style.opacity = "0";
          svg.style.opacity = "1";
        });
      };

      requestAnimationFrame(() => ensureAll());

      hoverTarget.addEventListener("mouseenter", onEnter);
      hoverTarget.addEventListener("mouseleave", onLeave);
    };

    init();
    document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", init);
  })();
})();</script> </a> <div class="flex flex-row justify-end" data-astro-cid-afdttjsk=""> <div class="hidden lg:flex" data-astro-cid-zqc3kjip=""><a href="/benchmarks" data-nav-id="benchmarks" data-astro-prefetch="" class="px-3 py-2 justify-center items-center gap-1 inline-flex transition-all duration-150 ease-out nav-button" data-astro-cid-zqc3kjip=""><div class="flex items-center gap-1" data-astro-cid-zqc3kjip=""><div class="nav-dot w-1 h-1 background-inverse-strong is-active" data-nav-id="benchmarks" aria-hidden="true" data-astro-cid-zqc3kjip=""></div><p class="font-medium tracking-tight text-xs/4 foreground-primary tracking-wide active:text-gravy-900 dark:active:text-neutral-50" data-astro-cid-zqc3kjip="">Benchmarks</p></div></a></div> <div class="hidden lg:flex" <style>astro-island,astro-slot,astro-static-slot{display:contents}</style><script data-astro-exec="">(()=>{var e=async t=>{await(await <script data-astro-exec="">
    (() => {
      let cleanup = null;
      const NAV_ACTIVE_ID_KEY = "vals-nav-active-id";
      const DOT_SWAP_DELAY_MS = 160;

      const initNav = () => {
        if (cleanup) cleanup();

        const mobileMenuContent = document.querySelector(
          ".mobile-menu-content",
        );
        const mobileMenuButton = document.querySelector(".mobile-menu-button");
        const hamburgerIcon = document.querySelector(".hamburger-icon");
        const mobileMenuContainer = document.querySelector(".navbar-mobile");

        let isExpanded = false;
        const toggleMenu = () => {
          if (
            !mobileMenuContent ||
            !mobileMenuButton ||
            !hamburgerIcon ||
            !mobileMenuContainer
          ) {
            return;
          }

          if (isExpanded) {
            mobileMenuContent.classList.remove(
              "h-full",
              "opacity-100",
              "max-h-auto",
            );
            mobileMenuContent.classList.add("max-h-0", "opacity-0");
            mobileMenuContainer.classList.remove("navbar-mobile-full-height");
            document.documentElement.style.overflow = "";
            document.body.style.overflow = "";
            hamburgerIcon.classList.remove("open");
            isExpanded = false;
          } else {
            mobileMenuContent.classList.remove("max-h-0", "opacity-0");
            mobileMenuContent.classList.add(
              "h-full",
              "opacity-100",
              "max-h-auto",
            );
            mobileMenuContainer.classList.add("navbar-mobile-full-height");
            document.documentElement.style.overflow = "hidden";
            document.body.style.overflow = "hidden";
            hamburgerIcon.classList.add("open");
            isExpanded = true;
          }
        };

        const playNavDotSequence = () => {
          const currentDot = document.querySelector(
            "#desktop-nav-shell .nav-dot.is-active",
          );
          const previousActiveId = sessionStorage.getItem(NAV_ACTIVE_ID_KEY);

          if (!currentDot) return;
          const currentActiveId = currentDot.getAttribute("data-nav-id");
          if (!currentActiveId) return;

          if (previousActiveId && previousActiveId !== currentActiveId) {
            const previousDot = document.querySelector(
              `#desktop-nav-shell .nav-dot[data-nav-id="${previousActiveId}"]`,
            );
            if (!previousDot) {
              sessionStorage.setItem(NAV_ACTIVE_ID_KEY, currentActiveId);
              return;
            }

            currentDot.classList.add("is-animatable");
            previousDot.classList.add("is-animatable");
            currentDot.classList.add("no-transition");
            previousDot.classList.add("no-transition");
            currentDot.classList.remove("is-active");
            previousDot.classList.add("is-active");

            void previousDot.offsetWidth;
            currentDot.classList.remove("no-transition");
            previousDot.classList.remove("no-transition");

            requestAnimationFrame(() => {
              previousDot.classList.remove("is-active");
              window.setTimeout(() => {
                currentDot.classList.add("is-active");
              }, DOT_SWAP_DELAY_MS);
            });
          } else {
            currentDot.classList.add("is-active");
          }

          sessionStorage.setItem(NAV_ACTIVE_ID_KEY, currentActiveId);
        };

        mobileMenuButton?.addEventListener("click", toggleMenu);
        playNavDotSequence();

        cleanup = () => {
          mobileMenuButton?.removeEventListener("click", toggleMenu);
          document.documentElement.style.overflow = "";
          document.body.style.overflow = "";
        };
      };

      initNav();
      document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", initNav);
      document.addEventListener("astro:before-swap", () => {
        if (cleanup) cleanup();
      });
    })();

    before:prose-p:content-none
    after:prose-p:content-none"> <h2 id="key-takeaways">Key Takeaways</h2>
<ul>
<li><a href="/models/anthropic_claude-opus-4-7" class="model-highlight">Claude Opus 4.7</a> is the current top performer on Finance Agent, scoring 64.37% accuracy. <a href="/models/anthropic_claude-sonnet-4-6" class="model-highlight">Claude Sonnet 4.6</a> follows with 63.33%, <a href="/models/meta_muse_spark" class="model-highlight">Muse Spark</a> with 60.59%, <a href="/models/deepseek_deepseek-v4-pro" class="model-highlight">DeepSeek V4</a> with 60.39%, and <a href="/models/anthropic_claude-opus-4-6-thinking" class="model-highlight">Claude Opus 4.6 (Thinking)</a> with 60.05%.</li>
<li>In the last six months, we’ve seen significant improvement on the benchmark - the capability of LLMs to take on financial tasks is dramatically increasing.</li>
<li>Models on average performed best in the simple quantitative and qualitative retrieval tasks. These tasks are easy but time-intensive for finance analysts.</li>
</ul>
<hr>
<h2 id="background">Background</h2>
<p>The frontier of applied AI is agents – systems that independently direct their own processes to maintain control over how they accomplish tasks on behalf of users [<a href="https://openai.com/index/new-tools-for-building-agents/">1</a>] [<a href="https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents">2</a>]. As such, foundation model labs have invested heavily in developing agents that can handle complex tasks [<a href="https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/mckinsey-digital/our-insights/why-agents-are-the-next-frontier-of-generative-ai">3</a>], making them prime candidates for delivering significant ROI in specialized industries [<a href="https://openai.com/index/new-tools-for-building-agents/">1</a>] [<a href="https://www.inc.com/ben-sherry/what-are-ai-agents-heres-how-they-can-help-you-get-stuff-done/91160443">4</a>].</p>
<p>Finance is one of the most lucrative applications of agents [<a href="https://www.ailaborindex.com/">5</a>], where AI has the potential to drive significant efficiency gains by performing tasks that mirror those of an entry-level financial analyst [<a href="https://www.mckinsey.com/business-functions/strategy-and-corporate-finance/our-insights/the-social-economy">6</a>]. Yet, there lacks a framework for evaluating these agents [<a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.01502">7</a>], highlighting the need for standardized evaluation methods to measure agentic ability on domain-specific tasks.</p>
<p>We have created a benchmark that tests the ability of agents to perform tasks expected of an entry-level financial analyst. In collaboration with Stanford researchers, a Global Systemically Important Bank, and industry experts, we created a dataset of 537 questions that evaluate skills such as simple retrieval, market research, and projections.</p>
<hr>
<h2 id="results">Results</h2>

<p>The Pareto cost-curve above illustrates the relationship between model accuracy and cost per session across different models. As model performance improves, these cost considerations will likely be more important.</p>
<p>Some challenging queries cost more than $5 for <a href="/models/openai_o3-2025-04-16" class="model-highlight">o3</a> or <a href="/models/anthropic_claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking" class="model-highlight">Claude Opus 4.1 (Thinking)</a>. Often, performance can be improved by allowing the model more tool calls and more tokens used, but this comes at a cost.</p>

<br>
<p>The tool call analysis reveals how many steps the models take to produce an answer. Interestingly, the top performers on this benchmark also register relatively high numbers of tool calls. This suggests that their comparatively strong performance on financial analyst tasks is partly due to leveraging the tools provided. It also reveals certain top models are making multiple tool calls per turn.</p>

<p>The spider graph detailing the number of tool calls performed by each model further supports the notion that the number of tool calls generally leads to higher accuracy. Again, we see several of the top models excelling by making many tool calls, especially for retrieving information, parse html_page, and Google web-search, which leads to top benchmark performance.</p>

Identify the relevant data\n   - From Netflix’s Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024 (Item 5, “Company Purchases of Equity Securities” – quarter ended December 31, 2024):\n     - October 2024: 519,883 shares\n     - November 2024: 457,732 shares\n     - December 2024: 188,212 shares\n     - These figures are both the **“Total number of shares purchased” (column (a))** and the **“Total number of shares purchased as part of publicly announced plans or programs” (column (c))**, i.e., there are no additional non‑program purchases.\n\n     Total Q4 2024 repurchases:\n     \\[\n     519{,}883 + 457{,}732 + 188{,}212 = 1{,}165{,}827 \\text{ shares}\n     \\]\n\n   - From Netflix’s Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2023 (Item 5, “Company Purchases of Equity Securities” – quarter ended December 31, 2023):\n     - October 2023: 287,360 shares\n     - November 2023: 2,708,477 shares\n     - December 2023: 2,481,771 shares\n     - Again, these are the same for columns (a) and (c), so they fully represent common stock repurchases under the program.\n\n     Total Q4 2023 repurchases:\n     \\[\n     287{,}360 + 2{,}708{,}477 + 2{,}481{,}771 = 5{,}477{,}608 \\text{ shares}\n     \\]\n\n2. Compute the percentage change in shares repurchased\nWe interpret the question as the standard percent change from Q4 2023 to Q4 2024:\n\\[\n\\%\\Delta = \\frac{\\text{Q4 2024 shares} - \\text{Q4 2023 shares}}{\\text{Q4 2023 shares}} \\times 100\n\\]\n\nPlug in the values:\n- Q4 2024 shares repurchased = 1,165,827\n- Q4 2023 shares repurchased = 5,477,608\n\nFirst compute the difference:\n\\[\n1{,}165{,}827 - 5{,}477{,}608 = -4{,}311{,}781\n\\]\nThen the percentage change:\n\\[\n\\%\\Delta = \\frac{-4{,}311{,}781}{5{,}477{,}608} \\times 100 \\approx -78.72\\%\n\\]\n\nInterpretation\n- Netflix repurchased **about 1.17 million** shares in Q4 2024 versus **about 5.48 million** shares in Q4 2023.\n- This represents a **78.72% decrease** in the total number of common stock shares repurchased in Q4 2024 compared with Q4 2023.\n\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix, Inc. December 2023: 2,481,771 shares\n\n### Calculation:\n% Change = (Q4 2024 Total - Q4 2023 Total) / Q4 2023 Total × 100\n% Change = (1,165,827 - 5,477,608) / 5,477,608 × 100\n% Change = -4,311,781 / 5,477,608 × 100\n% Change = **-78.72%**\n\n### Explanation:\nNetflix repurchased significantly fewer shares in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023 (a decrease of approximately 78.72%). October: 287,360 shares at $404.62 avg price\n  - November: 2,708,477 shares at $447.03 avg price\n  - December: 2,481,771 shares at $472.63 avg price\n\n**Q4 2024 (October-December 2024):**\n- Total shares repurchased: 1,165,827 shares\n- Monthly breakdown:\n  - October: 519,883 shares at $724.15 avg price\n  - November: 457,732 shares at $792.49 avg price\n  - December: 188,212 shares at $913.13 avg price\n\n### Calculation:\n% Change = ((Q4 2024 - Q4 2023) / Q4 2023) × 100\n% Change = ((1,165,827 - 5,477,608) / 5,477,608) × 100\n% Change = (-4,311,781 / 5,477,608) × 100\n% Change = **-78.73%**\n\n### Analysis:\nNetflix significantly reduced its share repurchase activity in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023, repurchasing 4,311,781 fewer shares (a 78.73% decrease). While the number of shares repurchased declined dramatically, it's worth noting that the average price per share more than doubled during this period (from approximately $404-$473 in Q4 2023 to $724-$913 in Q4 2024), which partially explains the reduced volume of repurchases.\n\nIn December 2024, Netflix's Board authorized an additional $15 billion for share repurchases, bringing the total remaining authorization to $17.1 billion as of December 31, 2024.\n\n### Sources:\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 10-K for fiscal year ended December 31, 2024\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 10-K for fiscal year ended December 31, 2023\&quot;\n        }\n    ]\n}\n&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,true],&quot;score&quot;:[0,1],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;grok/grok-4-1-fast-reasoning&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;**Step-by-Step Reasoning:**\n\n1. **Identify Relevant Filings:** Netflix's (NFLX, CIK 1065280) fiscal year ends December 31. Data for Q4 2024\nAccording to the 2024 Form 10-K, the monthly share repurchases for the fourth quarter of 2024 were:\n*   **October 1 – 31, 2024:** 519,883 shares\n*   **November 1 – 30, 2024:** 457,732 shares\n*   **December 1 – 31, 2024:** 188,212 shares\n*   **Total Q4 2024:** 1,165,827 shares\n\n### 2. Data for Q4 2023\nAccording to the 2023 Form 10-K, the monthly share repurchases for the fourth quarter of 2023 were:\n*   **October 1 – 31, 2023:** 287,360 shares\n*   **November 1 – 30, 2023:** 2,708,477 shares\n*   **December 1 – 31, 2023:** 2,481,771 shares\n*   **Total Q4 2023:** 5,477,608 shares\n\n### 3. Calculation of % Change\nThe percentage change is calculated using the following formula:\n\\[ \\text{Percentage Change} = \\left( \\frac{\\text{New Value} - \\text{Old Value}}{\\text{Old Value}} \\right) \\times 100 \\]\n\nPlugging in the numbers:\n*   **New Value (Q4 2024):** 1,165,827\n*   **Old Value (Q4 2023):** 5,477,608\n\n\\[ \\text{Change in Shares} = 1,165,827 - 5,477,608 = -4,311,781 \\]\n\\[ \\text{Percentage Change} = \\left( \\frac{-4,311,781}{5,477,608} \\right) \\times 100 \\]\n\\[ \\text{Percentage Change} \\approx -0.787164928 \\times 100 \\]\n\\[ \\text{Percentage Change} \\approx -78.7164928\\% \\]\n\nRounding to two decimal places, the percentage change is **-78.72%**.\n\n### Sources:\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Inc. 2024 Form 10-K (Annual Report)\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Inc. 2023 Form 10-K (Annual Report)\&quot;\n        }\n    ]\n}&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,true],&quot;score&quot;:[0,1],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;zai/glm-4.7&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;The % Change in the Total Number of Common Stock Shares Repurchased by Netflix (NASDAQ: NFLX) in Q4 2024 vs Q4 2023 is -78.73%.\n\n**Calculation Details:**\n\n- **Q4 2024 Total Shares Repurchased:** 1,165,827 shares\n  - This information is typically found in the company's quarterly (10-Q) or annual (10-K) filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).\n\n**Step 1: Find the number of shares repurchased in Q4 2023.**\n\nThis information is available in Netflix's 10-K filing for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2023. In Part II, Item 5 of this report, under the \&quot;Company Purchases of Equity Securities\&quot; table, the company provides a breakdown of share repurchases. For the fourth quarter of 2023 (three months ended December 31, 2023), the total number of shares repurchased is the sum of the repurchases in October, November, and December 2023:\n\n*   October 2023: 287,360 shares\n*   November 2023: 2,708,477 shares\n*   December 2023: 2,481,771 shares\n*   **Total Q4 2023:** 5,477,608 shares\n\n**Step 2: Find the number of shares repurchased in Q4 2024.**\n\nSimilarly, this information is found in Netflix's 10-K filing for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024. The \&quot;Company Purchases of Equity Securities\&quot; table in Part II, Item 5, shows the following for the fourth quarter of 2024:\n\n*   October 2024: 519,883 shares\n*   November 2024: 457,732 shares\n*   December 2024: 188,212 shares\n*   **Total Q4 2024:** 1,165,827 shares\n\n**Step 3: Calculate the percentage change.**\n\nThe percentage change is calculated using the following formula:\n\nPercentage Change = [(Q4 2024 Shares - Q4 2023 Shares) / Q4 2023 Shares] * 100\n\nPercentage Change = [(1,165,827 - 5,477,608) / 5,477,608] * 100\n\nPercentage Change = [-4,311,781 / 5,477,608] * 100\n\nPercentage Change = -0.78716 * 100\n\nPercentage Change = -78.72%\n\n**Conclusion:**\n\nThe total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix in Q4 2024 decreased by **78.72%** compared to Q4 2023.\n\n**Sources:**\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix, Inc. Price Paid |\n|-------|-------------------|----------------|\n| October 2024 | 519,883 | $724.15 |\n| November 2024 | 457,732 | $792.49 |\n| December 2024 | 188,212 | $913.13 |\n| **Q4 2024 Total** | **1,165,827** | — |\n\n**Q4 2023 Share Repurchases (from 2023 Annual Report / 10-K filed January 26, 2024):**\n| Month | Shares Repurchased | Avg. Price Paid |\n|-------|-------------------|----------------|\n| October 2023 | 287,360 | $404.62 |\n| November 2023 | 2,708,477 | $447.03 |\n| December 2023 | 2,481,771 | $472.63 |\n| **Q4 2023 Total** | **5,477,608** | — |\n\n### Calculation:\n% Change = (Q4 2024 − Q4 2023) / Q4 2023 × 100\n\n% Change = (1,165,827 − 5,477,608) / 5,477,608 × 100\n\n% Change = −4,311,781 / 5,477,608 × 100\n\n**% Change = −78.72%**\n\nNetflix repurchased approximately **78.72% fewer shares** in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023. This dramatic decline is largely explained by the significant increase in Netflix's stock price (from the ~$405–$473 range in Q4 2023 to the ~$724–$913 range in Q4 2024), meaning each dollar of buyback activity purchased far fewer shares.\n\n---\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 2024 Annual Report (Form 10-K) - Source: Netflix 2023 10-K, Item 5 (Page 19)\n  - Monthly breakdown: October (287,360) + November (2,708,477) + December (2,481,771) = 5,477,608\n\n- **Q4 2024 Common Stock Shares Repurchased:** 1,165,827 shares\n  - Source: Netflix 2024 10-K, Item 5 (Page 17)\n  - Monthly breakdown: October (519,883) + November (457,732) + December (188,212) = 1,165,827\n\n### Calculation:\nThe percentage change formula is:\n$$\\text{\\% Change} = \\frac{\\text{New Value} - \\text{Old Value}}{\\text{Old Value}} \\times 100$$\n\nWhere:\n- New Value (Q4 2024) = 1,165,827 shares\n- Old Value (Q4 2023) = 5,477,608 shares\n\n$$\\text{\\% Change} = \\frac{1,165,827 - 5,477,608}{5,477,608} \\times 100$$\n\n$$\\text{\\% Change} = \\frac{-4,311,781}{5,477,608} \\times 100$$\n\n$$\\text{\\% Change} = -0.7871627 \\times 100$$\n\n$$\\text{\\% Change} = -78.71627\\%$$\n\n### Final Answer:\n**-78.72%**\n\nThis represents a **78.72% decrease** in the total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023.\n\n### Sources:\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 2023 10-K Filing (SEC EDGAR)\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 2024 10-K Filing (SEC EDGAR)\&quot;\n        }\n    ]\n}&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,true],&quot;score&quot;:[0,1],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;zai/glm-5-thinking&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;## % Change in Netflix's Common Stock Shares Repurchased: Q4 2024 vs Q4 2023\n\n### Data Retrieved:\n- **Q4 2024 shares repurchased:** 1,165,827 shares\n  - December 31, 2024):\n* October 1 - 31, 2024: 519,883 shares\n* November 1 - 30, 2024: 457,732 shares\n* December 1 - 31, 2024: 188,212 shares\n* **Total Q4 2024 Repurchases:** 1,165,827 shares\n\n**Step 2: Determine the shares repurchased in Q4 2023**\nAccording to Netflix's 2023 Form 10-K (Item 5), the company repurchased the following number of shares during the fourth quarter (October 1 - December 31, 2023):\n* October 1 - 31, 2023: 287,360 shares\n* November 1 - 30, 2023: 2,708,477 shares\n* December 1 - 31, 2023: 2,481,771 shares\n* **Total Q4 2023 Repurchases:** 5,477,608 shares\n\n**Step 3: Calculate the Percentage Change**\nThe formula for percentage change is: \n`((New Value - Old Value) / Old Value) * 100`\n\n* Old Value (Q4 2023): 5,477,608\n* New Value (Q4 2024): 1,165,827\n\n`Percentage Change = ((1,165,827 - 5,477,608) / 5,477,608) * 100`\n`Percentage Change = (-4,311,781 / 5,477,608) * 100`\n`Percentage Change = -0.7871649... * 100`\n`Percentage Change = -78.72%`\n\nThe total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix decreased by **78.72%** in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023.\n\n```json\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 2024 Form 10-K\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 2023 Form 10-K\&quot;\n        }\n    ]\n}\n```&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,true],&quot;score&quot;:[0,1],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;To calculate the percentage change in the total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix (NFLX) in Q4 2024 versus Q4 2023, we first determine the total number of shares repurchased for each period based on the company's 10-K filings.\n\n### 1. Data Retrieval\nBased on the SEC 10-K filings for Netflix:\n\n**Q4 2024 (October 1 – December 31, 2024):**\n*   October 2024: 519,883 shares\n*   November 2024: 457,732 shares\n*   December 2024: 188,212 shares\n*   **Total Q4 2024:** 519,883 + 457,732 + 188,212 = **1,165,827 shares**\n\n**Q4 2023 (October 1 – December 31, 2023):**\n*   October 2023: 287,360 shares\n*   November 2023: 2,708,477 shares\n*   December 2023: 2,481,771 shares\n*   **Total Q4 2023:** 287,360 + 2,708,477 + 2,481,771 = **5,477,608 shares**\n\n### 2. Calculation\nThe percentage change is calculated using the formula:\n$$\\text{% Change} = \\left( \\frac{\\text{Total Q4 2024 Shares} - \\text{Total Q4 2023 Shares}}{\\text{Total Q4 2023 Shares}} \\right) \\times 100$$\n\nSubstituting the values:\n$$\\text{% Change} = \\left( \\frac{1,165,827 - 5,477,608}{5,477,608} \\right) \\times 100$$\n$$\\text{% Change} = \\left( \\frac{-4,311,781}{5,477,608} \\right) \\times 100$$\n$$\\text{% Change} \\approx -0.787164 \\times 100$$\n$$\\text{% Change} \\approx -78.72\\%$$\n\nThe total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023 decreased by **78.72%**.\n\n### Sources\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 2024 Annual Report (10-K)\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix 2023 Annual Report (10-K)\&quot;\n        }\n    ]\n}&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,true],&quot;score&quot;:[0,1],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;alibaba/qwen3.5-flash&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;The % Change in the Total Number of Common Stock Shares Repurchased by Netflix (NASDAQ: NFLX) in Q4 2024 vs Q4 2023 is **-78.72%**.\n\n**Calculation Details:**\n\n| Period | Shares Repurchased |\n|--------|-------------------|\n| Q4 2023 | 5,477,608 shares |\n| Q4 2024 | 1,165,827 shares |\n\n**Formula:** % Change = ((Q4 2024 - Q4 2023) / Q4 2023) × 100\n**Calculation:** ((1,165,827 - 5,477,608) / 5,477,608) × 100 = -78.72%\n\nThis represents a 78.72% decrease in share repurchases in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023.\n\n**Sources:**\n- Netflix Inc. Identified Netflix's quarterly shareholder letters (EX-99.1 from 8-K filings) as the primary source for share repurchase data, as these documents explicitly state the number of shares repurchased each quarter.\n\n2. Extracted data from the letters:\n   - Q4 2023: 5.5 million shares repurchased (quoted: \&quot;In Q4’23, we repurchased 5.5M shares for $2.5B\&quot;).\n   - Q1 2024: 3.6 million shares (quoted: \&quot;we repurchased 3.6M shares for $2B\&quot;).\n   - Q2 2024: 2.6 million shares (quoted: \&quot;we repurchased 2.6M shares for $1.6B\&quot;).\n   - Q3 2024: 2.6 million shares (quoted: \&quot;During Q3, we repurchased 2.6M shares for $1.7B\&quot;).\n   - Full year 2024: 9.9 million shares (quoted: \&quot;During 2024, we repurchased 9.9M shares for $6.2B\&quot;).\n\n3. Calculated Q4 2024 shares by subtraction: 9.9M - (3.6M + 2.6M + 2.6M) = 9.9M - 8.8M = 1.1 million shares.\n\n4. Percentage change formula: ((Q4 2024 shares - Q4 2023 shares) / Q4 2023 shares) × 100 = ((1.1 - 5.5) / 5.5) × 100 = (-4.4 / 5.5) × 100 = -80.00%.\n\nThe % change is -80.00%.\n\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000033/ex991_q424.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Q4 2024 Shareholder Letter\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000029/ex991_q423.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Q4 2023 Shareholder Letter\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000127/ex991_q124.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Q1 2024 Shareholder Letter\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000199/ex991_q224.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Q2 2024 Shareholder Letter\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000286/ex991_q324.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Q3 2024 Shareholder Letter\&quot;\n        }\n    ]\n}&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,false],&quot;score&quot;:[0,0.25],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;openai/gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;Netflix repurchased:\n- **Q4 2024:** **1,165,827** shares\n- **Q4 2023:** **5,477,608** shares\n\nPercent change (Q4 2024 vs Q4 2023) = **(1,165,827 - Securities\&quot; tables found in Item 5 of the company's 10-K filings.\n\n### 1. Data Retrieval\n*   **Common Stock Shares Repurchased in Q4 2024** (for the three months ended December 31, 2024):\n    *   October 2024: 519,883 shares\n    *   November 2024: 457,732 shares\n    *   December 2024: 188,212 shares\n    *   **Total Q4 2024: 1,165,827 shares**\n\n*   **Common Stock Shares Repurchased in Q4 2023** (for the three months ended December 31, 2023):\n    *   October 2023: 287,360 shares\n    *   November 2023: 2,708,477 shares\n    *   December 2023: 2,481,771 shares\n    *   **Total Q4 2023: 5,477,608 shares**\n\n### 2. Calculation\nThe percentage change is calculated as follows:\n$$\\text{Percentage Change} = \\left( \\frac{\\text{Shares}_{Q4\\ 2024} - \\text{Shares}_{Q4\\ 2023}}{\\text{Shares}_{Q4\\ 2023}} \\right) \\times 100$$\n$$\\text{Percentage Change} = \\left( \\frac{1,165,827 - 5,477,608}{5,477,608} \\right) \\times 100$$\n$$\\text{Percentage Change} = \\left( \\frac{-4,311,781}{5,477,608} \\right) \\times 100$$\n$$\\text{Percentage Change} \\approx -78.7137\\%$$\n\nRounding to two decimal places, the percentage change is **-78.71%**.\n\n**Final Answer:**\nThe total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix in Q4 2024 decreased by **78.71%** compared to Q4 2023.\n\n**Sources:**\n{\n    \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Inc. 2024 Form 10-K\&quot;\n        },\n        {\n            \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm\&quot;,\n            \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix Inc. 2023 Form 10-K\&quot;\n        }\n    ]\n}&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,true],&quot;score&quot;:[0,1],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;anthropic/claude-opus-4-7&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;## % Change in Total Common Stock Shares Repurchased by Netflix: Q4 2024 vs. Q4 2023\n\n### Data Gathered from Netflix's 10-K Filings (Issuer Purchases of Equity Securities)\n\n**Q4 2024 Share Repurchases** (from Netflix's 10-K for fiscal year 2024, filed January 27, 2025):\n| Month | Shares Repurchased | Avg. Change in Total Number of Common Stock Shares Repurchased by Netflix (NFLX): Q4 2024 vs Q4 2023\n\n### Data Extracted from Netflix's 10-K Filings:\n\n**Q4 2023 (October 1 – December 31, 2023):**\n\n| Month | Shares Repurchased |\n|---|---|\n| October 2023 | 287,360 |\n| November 2023 | 2,708,477 |\n| December 2023 | 2,481,771 |\n| **Total Q4 2023** | **5,477,608** |\n\n*(Source: Netflix 10-K for FY2023, filed January 26, 2024 — Item 5: Issuer Purchases of Equity Securities)*\n\n**Q4 2024 (October 1 – December 31, 2024):**\n\n| Month | Shares Repurchased |\n|---|---|\n| October 2024 | 519,883 |\n| November 2024 | 457,732 |\n| December 2024 | 188,212 |\n| **Total Q4 2024** | **1,165,827** |\n\n*(Source: Netflix 10-K for FY2024, filed January 27, 2025 — Item 5: Issuer Purchases of Equity Securities)*\n\n---\n\n### Calculation:\n\n**% Change = [(Q4 2024 – Q4 2023) / Q4 2023] × 100**\n\n= [(1,165,827 – 5,477,608) / 5,477,608] × 100\n\n= [–4,311,781 / 5,477,608] × 100\n\n= –0.787175… × 100\n\n= **–78.72%**\n\n---\n\n### Answer:\nThe total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix decreased by **78.72%** in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023.\n\n### Sources:\n- Netflix Inc. 2024 10-K (filed 2025-01-27): https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\n- Netflix Inc. 2023 10-K (filed 2024-01-26): https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm&quot;],&quot;is_correct&quot;:[0,true],&quot;score&quot;:[0,1],&quot;feedback&quot;:[0,&quot;&quot;]}],&quot;grok/grok-4.3&quot;:[0,{&quot;answer&quot;:[0,&quot;**Step-by-Step Calculation and Reasoning:**\n\nTo calculate the % change in the total number of common stock shares repurchased by Netflix in Q4 2024 versus Q4 2023, I retrieved and analyzed Netflix's SEC Form 10-K filings for the fiscal years ended December 31, 2024 (filed January 27, 2025) and December 31, 2023 (filed January 26, 2024) from the EDGAR database. These filings contain detailed disclosures on issuer purchases of equity securities in Item 5, including quarterly breakdowns for the fourth quarter, as well as supporting details in the MD&amp;A (Liquidity and Capital Resources), Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows, Consolidated Statements of Stockholders’ Equity, and Note 9 (Stockholders’ Equity).\n\n**Q4 2023 Repurchases (from 2023 10-K, Item 5 table):**\n- October 2023: 287,360 shares\n- November 2023: 2,708,477 shares\n- December 2023: 2,481,771 shares\n- **Total Q4 2023: 5,477,608 shares**\n\n**Q4 2024 Repurchases (from 2024 10-K, Item 5 table):**\n- October 2024: 519,883 shares\n- November 2024: 457,732 shares\n- December 2024: 188,212 shares\n- **Total Q4 2024: 1,165,827 shares**\n\nThese figures represent shares repurchased as part of publicly announced programs (open market and other compliant transactions under the authorized repurchase plans). No adjustments were needed for unsettled transactions, as the filings confirm all shares are settled. The numbers exclude any excise tax impacts, as the question asks specifically for the *number of shares* (not dollar value).\n\n**Percentage Change Calculation:**\n- Formula: % Change = [(Q4 2024 shares - Q4 2023 shares) / Q4 2023 shares] × 100\n- Difference: 1,165,827 - 5,477,608 = -4,311,781\n- Ratio: -4,311,781 / 5,477,608 = -0.7871645 (computed without rounding intermediates: exact division yields approximately 0.7871645 after verifying 5,477,608 × 0.7871645 ≈ 4,311,781)\n- % Change: -0.7871645 × 100 = -78.71645%\n- **Rounded final answer to two decimal places (as specified):** -78.72%\n\nThis represents a significant decrease in repurchase activity in Q4 2024 compared to Q4 2023, consistent with the overall annual trend (9.86 million shares in 2024 vs. 14.51 million in 2023) and the timing of the $15 billion authorization increase in December 2024 (which appears to have affected later activity or remaining capacity).\n\n**Sources:**\n{\n  \&quot;sources\&quot;: [\n    {\n      \&quot;url\&quot;: \&quot;https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm\&quot;,\n      \&quot;name\&quot;: \&quot;Netflix, Inc. Repurchased by Netflix: Q4 2024 vs. Q4 2023<br></span><span><br></span><span>### Data Gathered from Netflix's 10-K Filings (Issuer Purchases of Equity Securities)<br></span><span><br></span><span>**Q4 Price/Share |<br></span><span>|---|---|---|<br></span><span>| October 2024 | 519,883 | $724.15 |<br></span><span>| November 2024 | 457,732 | $792.49 |<br></span><span>| December 2024 | 188,212 | $913.13 |<br></span><span>| **Total Q4 2024** | **1,165,827** | |<br></span><span><br></span><span>**Q4 2023 Share Repurchases** (from Netflix's 10-K for fiscal year 2023, filed January 26, 2024):<br></span><span>| Month | Shares Repurchased | Avg. Price/Share |<br></span><span>|---|---|---|<br></span><span>| October 2023 | 287,360 | $404.62 |<br></span><span>| November 2023 | 2,708,477 | $447.03 |<br></span><span>| December 2023 | 2,481,771 | $472.63 |<br></span><span>| **Total Q4 2023** | **5,477,608** | |<br></span><span><br></span><span>### % Change Calculation<br></span><span><br></span><span>% Change = (Q4 2024 − Q4 2023) / Q4 2023 × 100<br></span><span>= (1,165,827 − 5,477,608) / 5,477,608 × 100<br></span><span>= −4,311,781 / 5,477,608 × 100<br></span><span>= **−78.72%**<br></span><span><br></span><span>### Answer<br></span><span>Netflix's total number of common stock shares repurchased in **Q4 2024 decreased by approximately 78.72%** compared to Q4 2023 (from 5,477,608 shares down to 1,165,827 shares). The decline was driven largely by significantly higher share prices in Q4 2024 (averaging $700–$900+ per share vs. $400–$475 per share in Q4 2023), resulting in fewer shares being repurchased despite continued buyback activity.<br></span><span><br></span><span>{<br></span><span>    "sources": [<br></span><span>        {<br></span><span>            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528025000044/nflx-20241231.htm",<br></span><span>            "name": "Netflix, Inc. Form 10-K for Fiscal Year Ended December 31, 2024 (Issuer Purchases of Equity Securities)"<br></span><span>        },<br></span><span>        {<br></span><span>            "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1065280/000106528024000030/nflx-20231231.htm",<br></span><span>            "name": "Netflix, Inc. Form 10-K for Fiscal Year Ended December 31, 2023 (Issuer Purchases of Equity Securities)"<br></span><span>        }<br></span><span>    ]<br></span><span>}<br></span></p><div class="flex flex-col lg:flex-row gap-2 items-start md:pl-6"><div class="background-accent-light px-2 py-1 flex flex-row justify-center items-center gap-1 "><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" class="lucide lucide-check-check w-4 h-4 foreground-accent" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M18 6 7 17l-5-5"></path><path d="m22 10-7.5 7.5L13 16"></path></svg><p class="foreground-accent text-2xs/4 font-normal font-mono uppercase tracking-wide">1</p></div><div class="foreground-primary text-sm/5 tracking-wide"><p></p></div></div></div></div></div></section></astro-island>
<p>The right answer we were looking for was “2024 - 1,165,827. 2023 - 5,477,608. The number of shares repurchased by the company decreased by 79% in 2024”. To answer this question, the finance expert did the following:</p>
<ul>
<li>Go to Edgar</li>
<li>Search Netflix Filings by Ticker</li>
<li>Identify the 10-K for 2024 and the 10-K for 2023</li>
<li>Search for Company Purchases of Equity Securities</li>
<li>Calculate the Total Number of Shares Purchased from Oct 1, 2024 - Dec 31, 2024</li>
<li>Calculate the Total Number of Shares Purchased from Oct 1, 2023 - Dec 31, 2023</li>
<li>Calculate the Percent Change in Total Number of Shares Purchased from 2023 to 2024</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="trajectory-comparison">Trajectory Comparison</h3>
<p>The visualizations below illustrate the sequence and types of tool calls made by two models for the example question above. Each node represents a step in the model’s reasoning process, with colored nodes indicating specific tool calls. These trajectories reveal how models approach research tasks, with more thorough tool usage often correlating with better performance.</p>
<p><a href="/models/anthropic_claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-thinking" class="model-highlight">Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Thinking)</a> trajectory:</p>
<p><img src="/benchmarks/fab/claude45-traj.png" alt="Claude 4.5 Sonnet trajectory"></p>
<p>We see that <a href="/models/anthropic_claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-thinking" class="model-highlight">Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Thinking)</a>‘s trajectory follows what we’d expect intuitively— an initial <code>edgar_search</code> (corresponding to going to EDGAR and searching for Netflix filings by ticker), followed
by <code>parse_html_page</code>, and finally <code>retrieve_information</code>.</p>
<p><a href="/models/google_gemini-2.5-pro-preview-03-25" class="model-highlight">Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview</a> trajectory:</p>
<p><img src="/benchmarks/fab/gemini25-traj.png" alt="Gemini 2.5 Pro trajectory"></p>
<p>In the case of <a href="/models/google_gemini-2.5-pro-preview-03-25" class="model-highlight">Gemini 2.5 Pro Preview</a>, it follows roughly the same pattern as <a href="/models/anthropic_claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-thinking" class="model-highlight">Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Thinking)</a>, except the model is
also able to recover from a failed tool call!</p>
<hr>
<h2 id="methodology">Methodology</h2>
<p>The finance industry comprises a wide array of tasks, but through consultation with experts at banks and hedge funds, we identified one core task shared across nearly all financial analyst workflows: performing research on the SEC filings of public companies. This task —while time-consuming— is foundational to activities such as equity research, credit analysis, and investment due diligence. We collaborated with industry experts to define a question taxonomy, write and review 537 benchmark questions.</p>
<p>The AI agents were evaluated in an environment where they had access to tools sufficient to produce an accurate response. This included an <a href="https://www.sec.gov/search-filings/edgar-search-assistance/using-edgar-research-investments">EDGAR search</a> interface via the SEC_API, Google search, a document parser (ParseHTML) for loading and chunking large filings, and a retrieval tool (RetrieveInformation) that enabled targeted questioning over extracted text. The human experts did not make use of any additional tools when writing and answering their questions. See the full harness <a href="https://github.com/vals-ai/finance-agent">here</a>.</p>
<p>Our primary evaluation metric was final answer accuracy (see the <a href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.12983">GAIA benchmark</a>). We also recorded latency, tool utilization patterns, and associated computational cost to provide a fuller picture of agent efficiency and practical viability. Together, these components form a rigorous and domain-specific evaluation framework for agentic performance in finance, advancing the field’s ability to measure and rely on AI in high-stakes settings.</p>

<p><img src="/benchmarks/fab/slide_LLM.png" alt="Page 1 of Edgar Research"></p>
<p>The code behind this harness is open source. Dive in and explore it yourself on <a href="https://github.com/vals-ai/finance-agent">this repo</a>!</p>
<h3 id="dataset">Dataset</h3>
<p>The dataset is divided into three parts: Public Validation (50 open-source samples), Private Validation (150 samples available for license), and Test (337 samples).</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>The Public Validation set is fully open and can be accessed at <a href="https://github.com/vals-ai/finance-agent/blob/main/data/public.csv">here</a>.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>The Private Validation set is available for license. Interested parties are encouraged to <a href="mailto:contact@vals.ai">contact us</a> directly for access.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>The Test set will remain private permanently. All results reported in this page are based solely on the Test set to prevent potential future overfitting.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p>The dataset splits were sampled to preserve the distribution of question types and performance characteristics. We observed a strong correlation in performance across the validation sets and the Test set, supporting the reliability of these splits.</p>
<hr>
<h2 id="question-taxonomy">Question Taxonomy</h2>
<h3 id="quantitative-retrieval-easy">Quantitative Retrieval (easy)</h3>
<p>Direct extraction of numerical information from one or more documents without any post-retrieval calculation or manipulation.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>What was the quarterly revenue of Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) for the quarter ended December 31, 2024?</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="qualitative-retrieval-easy">Qualitative Retrieval (easy)</h3>
<p>Direct quotation or summarization of non-numerical information from one or more documents.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Describe the product offerings and business model of Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)?</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="numerical-reasoning-easy">Numerical Reasoning (easy)</h3>
<p>Calculations or aggregation of key numbers to produce an answer.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>What is % of revenue derived from AWS in each year and the 3 year CAGR from 2021-2024 of Amazon?</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="complex-retrieval-medium">Complex Retrieval (medium)</h3>
<p>Numerical or non-numerical retrieval or content summarization requiring synthesis of information from multiple documents.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Please briefly summarize the most recent capital raise conducted by Viking Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VKTX).</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="adjustments-medium">Adjustments (medium)</h3>
<p>Quantitative and qualitative analysis of reporting context bridging GAAP and Non-GAAP Financial Metrics.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>What is Lemonade Insurance’s Adjusted EBITDA for the year ended December 31, 2024?</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="beat-or-miss-medium">Beat or Miss (medium)</h3>
<p>Comparison of forward management guidance versus actuals, synthesized by reconciling sequential quarterly reporting documents.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>How did Lam Research’s revenue compare to management projections (at midpoint) on a quarterly basis in 2024? Format as % BEAT or MISS. Use guidance provided on a quarterly basis.</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="trends-hard">Trends (hard)</h3>
<p>Analyze patterns within a single company’s reporting structure or calculate and contextualize evolving performance, key metrics or business composition.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Which Geographic Region has Airbnb (NASDAQ: ABNB) experienced the most revenue growth from 2022 to 2024?</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="financial-modeling-hard">Financial Modeling (hard)</h3>
<p>Complex numerical reasoning calculations which require additional financial expertise to define and evaluate.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>How much M&amp;A firepower does Amazon have as of FY2024 end including balance sheet cash, non-restricted cash and other short term investments, and up to 2x GAAP EBITDA leverage? Round to nearest billion</p>
</blockquote>
<h3 id="market-analysis-hard">Market Analysis (hard)</h3>
<p>Advanced analysis of one or more companies using various documents, requiring normalization of comparison metrics, or complex reasoning and usage of causality to contextualize drivers of business changes or competition dynamics.</p>
<blockquote>
<p>Compare the quarterly revenue growth of FAANG companies between 2022-2024.</p>
</blockquote>
<hr>
<h2 id="finance-agent-v11">Finance Agent v1.1</h2>
<p>At the beginning of 2026, we performed a refresh of the data, the harness, and the evaluation methodology to ensure the benchmark remains at an extremely high standard. The primary changes are as follows:</p>
<h3 id="data">Data</h3>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://afterquery.com">AfterQuery</a> ran quality control on the benchmark data using finance experts from leading global investment banks, private equity firms, and hedge funds, including Goldman Sachs, Silver Lake, and Citadel.</li>
<li>We switched many of the questions from relative dates (e.g., “current year”, “last three quarters”) to absolute dates (“April 2025”, “Q1–Q4 2024”). This is in addition to the pre-existing instructions in 1.0, which instruct the model to “answer each question as if today was 4/7/25.”</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="harness">Harness</h3>
<ul>
<li>The search tool was switched from Serp API to <a href="https://www.tavily.com/">Tavily</a>.</li>
<li>Model submission is now via a <code>submit</code> tool call rather than extracted from the message history.</li>
<li>The prompt template was updated to better instruct the model on including supporting reasoning and evidence in its final answer.</li>
<li>The CIK parameter in the SEC search is now optional.</li>
<li>Additional instructions on how to use the data storage were added to the prompt template.</li>
<li>The system prompt was also modified to instruct the model to give answers to two decimal places and not round any intermediate calculations.</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="evaluation">Evaluation</h3>
<ul>
<li>The evaluator model was upgraded to GPT-5.2.</li>
<li>The LLM-as-judge now uses the mode of three evaluations to reduce variance.</li>
<li>Clearer guidelines on dealing with rounding were added to the LLM-as-Judge prompt.</li>
</ul>
<p>All models were re-run from scratch using the FAB v1.1 harness and data.</p>

d="M352.35,31.23c-.43-1.47-.51-2.34-.26-2.6.48-.59,2.15-.42,2.61-.13,1.66,2.26,1.49,6.01,2.23,8.59.66,2.92.93,3.87,1.53,7.29.46,1.48-3.07,1.64-3.44.13-2.06-5.68-5.88-10.44-12.11-10.41-5.22-.11-8.97,3.62-8.92,8.72-.05,5.72,4.65,8.26,9.24,10.87,5.1,2.77,10.88,5.56,14.34,10.48,11.34,20.15-13.79,31.17-30.08,23.82-.1,1.8.21,2.03-1.53,2.47-.82.17-1.55.08-1.66-1.04-.72-6.66-1.78-10.36-2.55-17.31-.35-1.32,3.11-2.26,3.31-.78,2.39,7.27,6.63,14.92,15.3,14.71,9.2.09,13.47-9.22,7.58-16.2-5.61-6.14-15.66-8.15-20.78-14.97-3.6-4.32-3.39-13.11.06-17.51,5.4-7.42,16.71-8.95,25.11-6.12Z"></path> <circle class="cls-1 letter-period" cx="375.78" cy="85.63" r="5.09"></circle> <path class="cls-1 letter-second-a" d="M433.5,46.33c.59,7.61-3.38,28.05.25,33.06,1.52,2.29,6.62,2.41,7.97.46.98-1.67,3.07-1.96,3.12-.06-1.88,11.84-22.1,16.72-21.41-4.29h-.38c-2.81,8.3-11.09,14.9-20.14,14.71-6.35,0-11.55-3.03-11.47-9.76.31-13.24,18.07-20.57,31.36-24.86.69-8.46.31-21.3-10.58-21.34-8.28.02-12.41,6.42-11.22,14.71-2.03,2.06-5.38,1.48-7.71-.13-3.77-2.82-2.11-9.12,1.47-12.04,11.41-10.11,39.52-10.86,38.75,9.57ZM408.9,83.03c9.57-.94,13.51-13.03,13.89-23.69-7.83,3.09-13.92,5.74-17.78,11.52-2.72,4.29-2.75,12.37,3.89,12.17Z"></path> <path class="cls-1 letter-i" d="M450.55,88.76c-1.12-.29-1.15-3.45-.26-3.91,0,0,7.39,0,7.39,0,1.91,0,2.36-1.96,2.36-3.44,0-4.57.32-36.26,0-39.25-.45-4.18-.45-5.55-4.02-5.55,0,0-5.74,0-5.74,0-1.09-.12-1.51-3.74-.26-3.91,7.24.33,12.96-2.47,18.23-4.68,1.27,0,1.78.52,1.78,1.56-.08,23.45-.05,23.62,0,47.65,0,6.6-.43,7.62,2.68,7.62,0,0,6.25,0,6.25,0,1.2.1,1.42,3.41.26,3.64l-28.68.26Z"></path> <circle class="cls-1 letter-i-dot" cx="461.69" cy="17.53" r="6.26"></circle> </svg> <canvas id="vals-canvas-3z5pap" class="absolute inset-0 w-full h-full opacity-0 pointer-events-none" aria-hidden="true" style="image-rendering: pixelated;" width="77" height="16"></canvas> </span> <script data-astro-exec="">(function(){const svgId = "vals-logo-3z5pap";
const canvasId = "vals-canvas-3z5pap";
const wrapperId = "vals-logo-wrapper-3z5pap";
const maxSize = 6;
const duration = 800;

  (() => {
    const init = () => {
      const wrapper = document.getElementById(wrapperId);
      const svg = document.getElementById(svgId);
      const canvas = document.getElementById(canvasId);
      if (!wrapper || !svg || !canvas) return;

      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
      let srcW = 0,
        srcH = 0,
        dpr = 1;
      let textData = null,
        textSourceCanvas = null,
        iconCanvas = null;
      let srcFill = "black";
      let capturePromise = null;

      const easeInOut = (t) => (t < 0.5 ? 2 * t * t : -1 + (4 - 2 * t) * t);

      // Text letter x-regions in SVG viewBox (0 0 480 100), right-to-left:
      // i+i-dot, second-a, period, s, l, first-a, v
      const SEGMENTS = [
        { svgX: 444, svgW: 41 },
        { svgX: 385, svgW: 59 },
        { svgX: 367, svgW: 18 },
        { svgX: 313, svgW: 54 },
        { svgX: 279, svgW: 34 },
        { svgX: 225, svgW: 54 },
        { svgX: 152, svgW: 73 },
      ];
      const N_SEG = SEGMENTS.length;
      // Each segment takes 40% of t=0..1; stagger so last starts at t=0.6
      const segWidth = 0.4;
      const stagger = (1 - segWidth) / (N_SEG - 1); // ≈ 0.1

      const buildClone = () => {
        const clone = svg.cloneNode(true);
        clone.setAttribute("width", srcW);
        clone.setAttribute("height", srcH);
        const origEls = [...svg.querySelectorAll("[class]")];
        const cloneEls = [...clone.querySelectorAll("[class]")];
        origEls.forEach((el, i) => {
          const fill = getComputedStyle(el).fill;
          if (fill && fill !== "none") cloneEls[i].style.fill = fill;
        });
        return clone;
      };

      const renderClone = (clone) =>
        new Promise((resolve) => {
          const svgStr = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(clone);
          const blob = new Blob([svgStr], {
            type: "image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8",
          });
          const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
          const img = new Image();
          img.onload = () => {
            const off = document.createElement("canvas");
            off.width = srcW;
            off.height = srcH;
            off.getContext("2d").drawImage(img, 0, 0, srcW, srcH);
            URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
            resolve(off);
          };
          img.onerror = () => resolve(null);
          img.src = url;
        });

      const captureAll = async () => {
        const rect = svg.getBoundingClientRect();
        if (rect.width === 0) return;
        dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
        srcW = Math.round(rect.width * dpr);
        srcH = Math.round(rect.height * dpr);
        canvas.width = srcW;
        canvas.height = srcH;

        const clsEl = svg.querySelector(".cls-1");
        if (clsEl) srcFill = getComputedStyle(clsEl).fill;

        // Text-only: hide VA icon group
        const textClone = buildClone();
        const vaForText = textClone.querySelector("[clip-path]");
        if (vaForText) vaForText.setAttribute("visibility", "hidden");

        // Icon-only: hide text letters, remove clip so full VA mark renders
        const iconClone = buildClone();
        [
          ".letter-v",
          ".letter-a",
          ".letter-l",
          ".letter-s",
          ".letter-period",
          ".letter-second-a",
          ".letter-i",
          ".letter-i-dot",
        ].forEach((sel) => {
          const el = iconClone.querySelector(sel);
          if (el) el.setAttribute("visibility", "hidden");
        });
        const vaForIcon = iconClone.querySelector("[clip-path]");
        if (vaForIcon) vaForIcon.removeAttribute("clip-path");

        const [tc, iconCnv] = await Promise.all([
          renderClone(textClone),
          renderClone(iconClone),
        ]);
        textSourceCanvas = tc;
        textData = tc
          ? tc.getContext("2d").getImageData(0, 0, srcW, srcH).data
          : null;
        iconCanvas = iconCnv;
      };

      const ensureAll = () => {
        if (!capturePromise) capturePromise = captureAll();
        return capturePromise;
      };

      new MutationObserver(() => {
        capturePromise = null;
        textData = null;
        textSourceCanvas = null;
        iconCanvas = null;
      }).observe(document.documentElement, {
        attributes: true,
        attributeFilter: ["class"],
      });

      // t: 0→1. Segment i starts at t = i*stagger, completes over segWidth.
      // Each segment pixelates then fades out. VA icon stays crisp throughout.
      const FADE_START = 0.55; // fraction of segT when fade begins (after pixelation)
      const render = (t) => {
        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, srcW, srcH);

        // Base: crisp full text as foundation
        if (textSourceCanvas) ctx.drawImage(textSourceCanvas, 0, 0);

        // Overdraw each segment with pixelation + fade as it animates
        if (textData) {
          for (let si = 0; si < N_SEG; si++) {
            const segT = Math.max(
              0,
              Math.min((t - si * stagger) / segWidth, 1),
            );
            if (segT <= 0) continue;

            const p = easeInOut(segT);
            const bs = Math.max(1, Math.round((1 + (maxSize - 1) * p) * dpr));

            // Fade out the segment after FADE_START
            const segAlpha =
              segT >= FADE_START
                ? easeInOut(1 - (segT - FADE_START) / (1 - FADE_START))
                : 1;

            // Nothing to draw yet (still crisp with no fade)
            if (bs <= 1 && segAlpha >= 1) continue;

            const { svgX, svgW } = SEGMENTS[si];
            const x0 = Math.round((svgX / 480) * srcW);
            const x1 = Math.min(srcW, Math.round(((svgX + svgW) / 480) * srcW));

            // Clear base text in this region; blocks (or nothing) replace it
            ctx.clearRect(x0, 0, x1 - x0, srcH);

            if (segAlpha > 0.01 && bs > 1) {
              ctx.save();
              ctx.globalAlpha = segAlpha;
              ctx.fillStyle = srcFill;
              for (let y = 0; y < srcH; y += bs) {
                for (let x = x0; x < x1; x += bs) {
                  let a = 0,
                    n = 0;
                  for (let dy = 0; dy < bs && y + dy < srcH; dy++) {
                    for (
                      let dx = 0;
                      dx < bs && x + dx < x1 && x + dx < srcW;
                      dx++
                    ) {
                      a += textData[((y + dy) * srcW + (x + dx)) * 4 + 3];
                      n++;
                    }
                  }
                  if (n > 0 && a / n > 102)
                    ctx.fillRect(x, y, Math.min(bs, x1 - x), bs);
                }
              }
              ctx.restore();
            }
            // segAlpha ≤ 0.01: region cleared, letter is gone
          }
        }

        // Icon always crisp on top
        if (iconCanvas) ctx.drawImage(iconCanvas, 0, 0);
      };

      const hoverTarget = wrapper.closest("a") ?? wrapper;
      let animFrame = null;
      let animT = 0;

      const animateTo = (target, onDone) => {
        if (animFrame) cancelAnimationFrame(animFrame);
        const from = animT;
        const dist = Math.abs(target - from);
        if (dist < 0.001) {
          render(animT);
          return onDone?.();
        }
        const dur = duration * dist;
        const start = performance.now();
        const tick = (now) => {
          const rawT = Math.min((now - start) / dur, 1);
          animT = from + (target - from) * rawT;
          render(animT);
          if (rawT < 1) {
            animFrame = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
          } else {
            animT = target;
            onDone?.();
          }
        };
        animFrame = requestAnimationFrame(tick);
      };

      const onEnter = async () => {
        await ensureAll();
        svg.style.opacity = "0";
        canvas.style.opacity = "1";
        animateTo(1);
      };

      const onLeave = () => {
        animateTo(0, () => {
          canvas.style.opacity = "0";
          svg.style.opacity = "1";
        });
      };

      requestAnimationFrame(() => ensureAll());

      hoverTarget.addEventListener("mouseenter", onEnter);
      hoverTarget.addEventListener("mouseleave", onLeave);
    };

    init();
    document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", init);
  })();
})();</script> </a> <astro-island uid="ZCI0WP" prefix="r12" component-url="/_astro/MailingList.Cy-ee3eN.js" component-export="default" renderer-url="/_astro/client.Dw0yIbNp.js" props="{&quot;data-astro-transition-persist&quot;:[0,&quot;mailing-list&quot;]}" client="load" opts="{&quot;name&quot;:&quot;MailingList&quot;,&quot;value&quot;:true}" data-astro-transition-persist="mailing-list" await-children=""><section class="flex w-full"><div class="flex flex-col gap-2"><p class="foreground-primary text-sm/4 tracking-wide">Join our mailing list to receive benchmark updates</p><div class="flex flex-row gap-2"><form class="flex items-center text-sm w-full"><input type="email" name="email" placeholder="your@email.com" class=" text-sm leading-4 w-60 p-2 rounded-sm background-tertiary focus:background-primary foreground-primary" required="" value=""></form><button class="px-3 py-2 button background-inverse-strong hover:background-inverse foreground-inverse-strong hover:foreground-inverse justify-center items-center flex text-2xs/4">Subscribe</button></div><p class="foreground-secondary text-xs/4 tracking-wide">By subscribing, I agree to the Vals<!-- --> <a href="https://www.platform.vals.ai/privacy/Product_Privacy_Statement.pdf" target="_blank" class="foreground-primary hover:underline font-mono uppercase text-2xs/4 traacking-tight">Privacy Policy</a>.</p></div></section></astro-island> <div class="flex-col justify-start items-start gap-2 md:gap-4 inline-flex"> <div class="flex-col justify-start items-start gap-2 flex"> <p class="foreground-secondary text-2xs/4 tracking-wider px-2 md:px-0">

src="https://us-assets.i.posthog.com/array/phc_vHLy7tfPfd5cjhb8lE77mFClZgjwaXa81wckZ1fEHkS/config.js"></script><script data-astro-exec="">
      (function () {
        function syncFooterPad() {
          var footer = document.getElementById("site-footer");
          var wrap = document.getElementById("main-content-wrap");
          if (!footer || !wrap) return;
          wrap.style.setProperty(
            "--footer-fixed-offset",
            Math.max(0, footer.offsetHeight - 1) + "px",
          );
        }
        function scheduleSync() {
          requestAnimationFrame(function () {
            requestAnimationFrame(syncFooterPad);
          });
        }
        scheduleSync();
        window.addEventListener("resize", scheduleSync);
        document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", scheduleSync);
        var footer = document.getElementById("site-footer");
        if (footer && typeof ResizeObserver !== "undefined") {
          new ResizeObserver(scheduleSync).observe(footer);
        }
      })();
    </script> <script data-astro-exec="">
      (function () {
        function fixHashLinks() {
          document.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]').forEach(function (link) {
            link.addEventListener("click", function (e) {
              var hash = link.getAttribute("href");
              if (!hash || hash === "#") return;
              var target = document.querySelector(hash);
              if (target) {
                e.preventDefault();
                target.scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth" });
                history.pushState(null, "", hash);
              }
            });
          });
        }
        fixHashLinks();
        document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", fixHashLinks);
      })();
    </script>   </body></html>
